purpose: Judge portrait uploads for face presence, multiplicity, size, sharpness, lighting, occlusion, and embedding readiness before generation workflows continue.

endpoints.description: Evaluate one portrait upload using face detection, face scoring, and optional embedding generation.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Portrait upload quality gate based on face and image quality signals.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use after receiving a portrait photo and before preview or final generation jobs are queued.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for forensic identity verification, biometric matching across people, or generic document image QA.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Decode upload bytes. 2) Run /portrait-uploads/judge. 3) Accept only when status is accepted and quality score meets policy.

failure_modes.condition: No face, multiple faces, low sharpness, poor light, or high occlusion
failure_modes.symptom: status rejected with a specific rejection_reason
failure_modes.recovery: Ask the user for a clearer, single-subject portrait and retry

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Compose a face detector, face scoring provider, and optional embedder. Reject when zero or multiple faces are detected, area_ratio is too small, sharpness is too low, light balance is too low, or occlusion exceeds threshold.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: A face detector can succeed while the quality score still makes the image unsuitable for generation. Keep thresholds explicit and test deterministic fake providers.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Return concrete rejection reasons such as no_face, multiple_faces, face_too_small, low_sharpness, low_light, or occlusion.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not silently accept borderline uploads and do not mix this module with payment or session lifecycle logic.
